summary: Legendary China>Crazy>Rider and monster Playing In The Band
blurb: The show delivers a jaw‑dropping China Cat Sunflower > Crazy Fingers > I Know You Rider sequence and a soaring, chaotic Playing In The Band jam. Jerry’s guitar soars while Phil lays down thunderous bass, and Brent’s organ drives the improvisations. Highlights also include tight Blow Away, heartfelt Believe It Or Not, and an uplifting Black Muddy River encore.
review: On July 29 1988 at Laguna Seca the Dead turned in one of the summer tour’s most memorable performances. The first set rolled solidly with Althea, Blow Away and a tight Deal, but it was the second set that truly ignited the crowd: a flawless China Cat Sunflower > Crazy Fingers > I Know You Rider segue that flowed straight into an explosive Playing In The Band, followed by a powerful Drums/Space and a high‑water Wheel > Gimme Some Lovin' stretch. Phil’s bass thundered through every jam, Brent’s Hammond organ filled the sonic space with both power and occasional over‑drive, and Jerry’s guitar sang “god‑like” on the climactic jams while delivering an emotive Black Muddy River encore. The recording landscape is rich—crystal‑clear soundboard tapes capture every nuance, while audience and matrix sources add their own vibrant ambience—but the Hayum SBD (gd88-07-29.sbd.hayum.5395.sbeok.shnf) stands out as the definitive listening experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across the majority of recordings (six out of eight labeled positive, one mixed). Reviewers repeatedly praise the China>Crazy>Rider sequence, Playing In The Band jam, and overall band tightness, with no consistent negative remarks about performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture that highlights the monster Playing In The Band and legendary China>Crazy>Rider sequence with superb instrument separation.

key_highlights: Monster Playing In The Band jam with thunderous bass and soaring guitar
Legendary China Cat Sunflower > Crazy Fingers > I Know You Rider segue
Tight, energetic Blow Away and heartfelt Believe It Or Not

band_performance.Jerry: God‑like guitar work and soaring vocals, especially on Playing In The Band; occasional early miscues but overall emotive late‑era playing.
band_performance.Phil: Thunderous, mile‑wide bass that drives the jams; highlighted on Blow Away, The Wheel > Gimme Some Lovin', and INYR.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals praised as wonderful and energetic by many, though a few early comments noted shoddy moments.
band_performance.Brent: Hammond organ propels the improvisations, heavy in the mix; at times too loud or tonally inconsistent, but thrashing brilliantly on Crazy Fingers and Playin'.
band_performance.Mickey: Explosive pre‑drums and tight playing during China Cat Sunflower; drums/space described as psychotic and thunderous.
band_performance.Billy: Same explosive energy in the pre‑drums, providing a solid rhythmic foundation throughout the second set.
